title: Primitive Types 2
description: The `char` type in Rust represents a Unicode Scalar Value, which means it can represent more than just ASCII.
instructions: Fix the code by creating the missing char variables.
hints: You need to declare a `char` variable called `your_character`.
Char literals in Rust use single quotes, like 'A'.
explanation: The `char` type in Rust is four bytes in size and represents a Unicode Scalar Value. This means it can represent much more than just ASCII - including Chinese characters, emoji, and zero-width spaces. Char literals are specified with single quotes, while string literals use double quotes.
